coreCompetencies: Understanding of the purpose of each Scrum event
Ability to take notes and track action items during ceremonies
Basic knowledge of how to keep a task board up to date
Familiarity with the difference between velocity and capacity
Ability to recognize an impediment when a team member raises one
Understanding of the roles of product owner, developers and Scrum Master
Basic notions of timeboxing and why events have fixed durations
Documentation of retrospective outcomes and follow-up items
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with a work-tracking tool such as Jira
Basic knowledge of burndown charts
Understanding of the definition of done
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Supports the facilitation of ceremonies
Is learning the fundamentals of Scrum

coreCompetencies: Facilitation of daily standups keeping them within the timebox
Tracking of impediments to resolution and escalation when blocked
Preparation and facilitation of sprint planning with the product owner
Reading of burndown and velocity trends to spot delivery risk
Support of backlog refinement so items are ready for planning
Facilitation of retrospectives that produce concrete action items
Protection of the team from mid-sprint scope changes
Reinforcement of the definition of done as a quality gate
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of cycle time as a complement to velocity
Understanding of work-in-progress limits
Familiarity with story splitting techniques
indicators: Facilitates events for a single team with support
Removes routine impediments independently
Tracks flow metrics for the team

coreCompetencies: Facilitation of the full Scrum event cycle for a team without support
Systematic removal of impediments, including cross-team dependencies
Use of cycle time and work-in-progress limits to improve flow
Coaching of the team on self-organization and healthy conflict
Diagnosis of retrospective anti-patterns and facilitation of real improvement
Reading of team health signals beyond velocity numbers
Coaching of the product owner on backlog readiness and sizing
Facilitation of estimation without letting velocity become a target
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of facilitation techniques for divergent and convergent thinking
Understanding of dependency mapping across teams
Familiarity with team maturity models
indicators: Owns the process for a team end to end
Improves flow metrics measurably over sprints
Coaches the team toward self-organization

coreCompetencies: Coaching of a mature team toward sustained self-organization
Resolution of systemic impediments that recur across multiple sprints
Facilitation of dependency management between two or three interfacing teams
Use of flow metrics to expose bottlenecks and rebalance work-in-progress
Facilitation of difficult conversations and team conflict resolution
Coaching of the product owner on prioritization and stakeholder alignment
Design of retrospectives that drive measurable change over time
Protection of team health and sustainable pace under delivery pressure
complementaryCompetencies: Experience facilitating cross-team planning sessions
Knowledge of Kanban as a complement to Scrum
Understanding of psychological safety as a team performance factor
indicators: Coaches a high-performing team
Resolves systemic impediments across sprints
Facilitates dependencies with neighboring teams

coreCompetencies: Coaching of multiple teams while keeping each team's autonomy intact
Facilitation of scaled planning across a group of interdependent teams
Critical application of a scaling framework such as Nexus or LeSS where it fits
Design of a flow-metrics dashboard that surfaces delivery risk early
Mentoring of junior Scrum Masters on facilitation and impediment removal
Resolution of impediments that require negotiation with other departments
Establishment of communities of practice around agile ways of working
Facilitation of team-of-teams retrospectives that improve the whole flow
complementaryCompetencies: Experience with dependency management at the program level
Knowledge of the trade-offs between Nexus, LeSS and SAFe
Understanding of change resistance at team level
indicators: Coaches several teams at once
Mentors junior Scrum Masters
Facilitates scaled events across teams

coreCompetencies: Leadership of the Scrum Master discipline across a business area
Design of the flow-metrics model used to steer delivery across teams
Critical evaluation of when to scale with a framework and when not to
Coaching of team leads and engineering managers on agile leadership
Removal of organizational impediments rooted in structure or policy
Facilitation of alignment between product and engineering at program level
Definition of hiring and growth paths for Scrum Masters
Establishment of sustainable pace as a norm across multiple teams
complementaryCompetencies: Experience redesigning team topologies to reduce dependencies
Knowledge of value stream mapping across a delivery organization
Understanding of incentive structures that undermine agility
indicators: Leads the Scrum Master practice for an area
Removes organizational impediments beyond team level
Coaches engineering and team leads

coreCompetencies: Direction of agile ways of working across a large delivery organization
Definition of the metrics that leadership uses to judge delivery health
Critical stewardship of scaling frameworks against real organizational needs
Coaching of senior leaders on the behaviors that enable team autonomy
Removal of impediments embedded in budgeting and governance processes
Facilitation of large-scale planning across many interdependent teams
Establishment of the growth and certification path for the discipline
Alignment of delivery cadence with strategic planning at company level
complementaryCompetencies: Experience shaping quarterly planning across a program of teams
Knowledge of organizational design for flow efficiency
Understanding of how governance affects delivery cadence
indicators: Directs agile delivery across an organization
Advises senior leadership on delivery health
Owns the growth path for the discipline

coreCompetencies: Definition of how agile delivery is measured and steered across the enterprise
Framing of team autonomy and flow efficiency as strategic operating choices
Critical position on scaling frameworks that resists cargo-cult adoption
Coaching of executives on the structural changes that unblock delivery
Removal of impediments rooted in the company's operating and funding model
Definition of the enterprise standard for healthy, sustainable delivery
Establishment of the Scrum Master discipline as a recognized career track
Alignment of delivery systems with the company's strategic cadence
complementaryCompetencies: Advisory on agile operating models across the organization
Influence on how the company funds and governs delivery
Track record of improving flow at organizational scale
indicators: Shapes enterprise delivery ways of working
Advises executives on delivery and flow
Is a recognized authority on agile delivery

coreCompetencies: Ownership of the enterprise operating model for agile delivery
Definition of the delivery-health metrics that govern the organization
Setting of the company's principled stance on scaling and team autonomy
Coaching of the executive team on the leadership behaviors that enable flow
Removal of the deepest structural impediments to delivery at company level
Establishment of sustainable delivery as an enduring organizational norm
Direction of the discipline's role and growth across the whole company
Alignment of the delivery system with long-term business strategy
complementaryCompetencies: Recognition as a leader in agile delivery across the industry
Influence on delivery practices beyond the company
Track record of leading large agile transformations to lasting effect
indicators: Leads agile delivery for the whole enterprise
Defines the organization's delivery operating model
Is a reference authority in agile delivery
